title: [testing-metamorphic] Series::str.find/rfind/index_of/rindex_of return BYTE position, not CHAR position (pandas divergence on multi-byte UTF-8)
description:
pandas semantics: Series.str.find/rfind/index/rindex return CHAR-based positions (Python 3 strings are char-indexed).

fp-frame Series.str.find/rfind/index_of/rindex_of (crates/fp-frame/src/lib.rs:14214,14227,14241,14255) return BYTE positions from Rust str::find/rfind. ASCII inputs are correct; multi-byte UTF-8 inputs diverge.

Repro:
  pandas:    pd.Series(["héllo"]).str.find("lo") -> 2  (char index)
  fp-frame:  s.str.find("lo") -> 3                       (byte index, é is 2 bytes)

Skill: /testing-metamorphic — cross-implementation oracle property: s.chars().nth(find_pos) starts the matched substring.

Fix: convert byte position to char position via &s[..byte_pos].chars().count().

title: [testing-conformance-harnesses] Live pandas oracle missing op_series_str_find/op_series_str_rfind — recently-fixed char-position bug has no live-oracle regression net
description:
Skill: /testing-conformance-harnesses.

br-frankenpandas-02ae2b just fixed Series.str.find/rfind to return CHAR (not BYTE) positions, matching pandas. The fix has 6 in-crate regression tests but no live-pandas oracle coverage. The conformance harness already supports SeriesStrFind/SeriesStrRfind FixtureOperations (lib.rs:676, 678, 15974, 15981) and the fixture struct has str_sub field, but the python oracle pandas_oracle.py has NO op_series_str_find or op_series_str_rfind handlers — dispatch() falls through to OracleError(unsupported operation).

Effect: any future regression that re-introduces byte/char divergence would only be caught by the in-crate tests with hand-coded expected values. A live oracle test would compare against the running pandas installation, the most authoritative parity check.

Fix:
1. Add op_series_str_find(pd, payload) and op_series_str_rfind(pd, payload) to pandas_oracle.py
2. Wire into dispatch()
3. Add live_oracle_series_str_find_char_position_matches_pandas + rfind variant in fp-conformance tests, with multi-byte UTF-8 inputs (héllo, café_lo, etc.) so a byte-position regression would diverge from pandas char-position output.

title: [SeriesGroupBy] add grouped expanding reductions
description: Implement a narrow SeriesGroupBy expanding window surface without taking over the full SeriesGroupBy umbrella. Required output: add SeriesGroupBy::expanding(min_periods) and a grouped expanding helper that applies existing Series expanding reductions per group, preserving the original flat Series index; expose sum/mean/min/max/std/count/var methods; add inline tests for per-group reset semantics, min_periods behavior, and original index preservation; validate with scoped fp-frame test/check/clippy/fmt plus UBS on touched Rust. Parent umbrella: frankenpandas-nt65g.
close_reason: Implemented SeriesGroupBy expanding reductions with min_periods/count semantics and focused validation.

title: [testing-fuzzing] No proptest for Series::cov_with_options symmetry + self-cov invariants
close_reason: Shipped in f2ec6d2: added proptests for Series::cov_with_options symmetry and self-covariance invariants.

title: [testing-metamorphic] No proptest asserting cumsum/diff round-trip property
description:
Skill: /testing-metamorphic.

Series::cumsum and Series::diff are inverses (modulo the first-position
NaN that diff inserts). For any null-free numeric series s of length n:

  cumsum(s).diff(1)[i] == s[i]  for i in [1, n)
  cumsum(s).diff(1)[0] == NaN

Existing proptests cover scaling linearity (prop_series_cumsum_scales_linearly
at line 9275 + diff variants near 6053) but no round-trip property test
asserts the cumsum/diff inverse relationship.

A regression that broke either op (e.g. diff drift on Float64 promotion,
cumsum off-by-one, mishandled boundary) would silently produce wrong
intermediate values that the existing scaling tests might not catch on
specific f64 inputs.

Add proptest with random null-free Float64 series of length 2..=12,
restricted to bounded magnitudes to avoid f64 catastrophic cancellation
near zero crossings.

title: [testing-golden-artifacts] DataFrame::to_json(records) no golden coverage
description: DataFrame::to_json("records") now has checked-in byte-for-byte golden artifact coverage for mixed dtypes, escaping, null/NaN, and bool/int/float rendering.
close_reason: Shipped fp-frame DataFrame::to_json("records") mixed-dtype golden artifact and assert_text_golden regression.

title: Add Period/Range Index slice_indexer
description: Reality-check finding artifacts/api-coverage-drift-2026-05-08.md: PeriodIndex/RangeIndex still miss slice_indexer. Returns std::ops::Range<usize> wrapping slice_locs. Single PR with regression tests.
close_reason: Shipped in ca28249: PeriodIndex/RangeIndex now expose pandas-parity slice_indexer. 1 regression test passes; 333 fp-index tests total.

title: [testing-golden-artifacts] DataFrame::to_markdown formatting has no golden-byte regression coverage
description:
Skill: /testing-golden-artifacts.

DataFrame::to_markdown (crates/fp-frame/src/lib.rs:25954) has rich formatting:
- Per-column width based on max(header, max(cell)) clamped to >= 3
- Float rendering: whole numbers get .1 padding (2.0 not 2), large/non-whole
  use Float64::to_string default
- NaN/null → "NaN" string
- Bool → "True"/"False"
- Index column inserted with empty header when include_index=true

Existing goldens cover only Series::Display and DataFrame::Display (basic
tabular). to_markdown's formatting could regress silently — e.g., width
clamping changing from 3 to something else, or float formatting drifting.

Fix: add dataframe_to_markdown_mixed.txt golden that captures the output
for a representative DataFrame (Int64, Float64 whole + non-whole, Utf8,
Bool, Null, Timedelta). Test asserts byte-equal output via existing
assert_text_golden helper (UPDATE_GOLDENS env var supported).

title: Add Datetime/Timedelta Index shift
description: Reality-check finding artifacts/api-coverage-drift-2026-05-08.md: typed Index variants miss shift. For tz-naive Datetime/Timedelta, shift(periods, freq_nanos) adds periods * freq_nanos to each label. NAT propagates as NAT. Single PR with regression tests.
close_reason: Shipped in 3a93bda: Datetime/Timedelta Index now expose pandas-parity shift(periods, freq_nanos) with saturating arithmetic and NAT propagation. 1 regression test passes; 340 fp-index tests total.

title: Reject zero repair-symbol encoded artifacts in fp-runtime codec
description: Fallback scan after an empty ready queue. PassthroughCodec::encode rejects max_repair_symbols == 0, but PassthroughCodec::decode accepts externally supplied EncodedArtifact values with repair_symbols == 0 as long as source_len <= encoded_bytes.len(). That creates an inconsistent repair-symbol manifest boundary for Asupersync/RaptorQ sidecar durability: artifacts the local encoder would never emit are accepted during decode/recovery. Fix by making decode fail closed when encoded.repair_symbols == 0, preserving the existing source_len guard, and add focused fp-runtime tests covering encode rejection, decode rejection, and a valid round trip.
close_reason: Added fail-closed PassthroughCodec::decode guard for zero repair-symbol encoded artifacts, replaced guarded direct slicing with checked range access, and added focused asupersync feature tests for encode rejection, decode rejection, and valid round trip.

title: Add fp-io read_fwf fixed-width file reader (explicit colspecs/widths)
description:
Reality-check finding (artifacts/api-coverage-drift-2026-05-08.md): IOWrappers is missing read_fwf without an open child bead.

Pandas signature:
  pd.read_fwf(filepath_or_buffer, colspecs='infer', widths=None, infer_nrows=100, ...)

Phase A scope: explicit colspecs OR widths, has_headers control, and the same NA / dtype / index_col options as read_csv. Auto-inference (colspecs='infer') is a follow-up.

Add:
- struct FwfReadOptions { colspecs: Option<Vec<(usize, usize)>>, widths: Option<Vec<usize>>, has_headers: bool, na_values, keep_default_na, na_filter, index_col, ... }
- read_fwf_str(input, options) parses each line into fixed-width slices, trims trailing whitespace, runs the same scalar-coercion path as read_csv.
- read_fwf(path, options) and read_fwf_str (no-options) convenience wrappers.
- Reject when both colspecs and widths are missing (Phase A defers infer mode); reject when both are provided to mirror pandas TypeError.

Single PR. Add regression tests covering colspecs parse, widths-derived colspecs, header inference, NA handling parity, and the deferred-infer rejection.
close_reason: Shipped in 94f3a50: fp-io exposes read_fwf_str/read_fwf with FwfReadOptions (colspecs|widths, has_headers, na_values, keep_default_na, na_filter, index_col, usecols, nrows, skiprows, dtype, parse_dates, true/false_values, decimal, thousands, skipfooter). IoError::Fwf added. Auto-infer mode deferred. 6 regression tests pass; 523 fp-io tests total.

title: Add fp-io reject-closed surfaces for read_clipboard/read_gbq/read_sas/read_spss
description:
Reality-check finding (artifacts/api-coverage-drift-2026-05-08.md): IOWrappers gap closes once read_clipboard, read_gbq, read_sas, read_spss are exposed. Each is out-of-scope for FrankenPandas's local-IO charter:

* read_clipboard — pulls from the OS clipboard; not implementable in a headless Rust crate without GUI bindings.
* read_gbq — calls Google BigQuery; would pull in GCP credentials + auth, outside the file-format scope.
* read_sas / read_spss — proprietary statistical-software formats; pandas implements them via pyreadstat / sas7bdat. No first-party Rust reader exists at parity.

Mirrors the deferred surface precedent set by frankenpandas-hci9o (plotting): expose typed compatibility-rejection functions so downstream code can program against the surface and fall through to a clean error rather than a missing symbol. Add:

- read_clipboard() -> Result<DataFrame, IoError>
- read_gbq(query: &str, project: Option<&str>) -> Result<DataFrame, IoError>
- read_sas(path: &Path) -> Result<DataFrame, IoError>
- read_spss(path: &Path) -> Result<DataFrame, IoError>

Each returns IoError::Fwf-style typed rejection (or a new IoError::Deferred variant if cleaner) with a pandas-leaning message. Add regression tests asserting each surface rejects with the expected message; update fp-io module docs to point readers at the deferral note. Single PR.
close_reason: Shipped in f9bc013: fp-io exposes read_clipboard/read_gbq/read_sas/read_spss as reject-closed surfaces backed by IoError::Deferred(String). Mirrors plotting deferral precedent (hci9o). 4 regression tests pass; 527 fp-io tests total.

title: Add Datetime/Timedelta Index std reduction
description: Reality-check finding artifacts/api-coverage-drift-2026-05-08.md: typed Index variants miss std. Compute sample standard deviation (ddof=1 by default) over non-NAT nanoseconds in f64 and return Option<i64>. Single PR with regression tests.
close_reason: Shipped in d337b2f: Datetime/Timedelta Index now expose pandas-parity std (sample, ddof=1). 1 regression test passes; 336 fp-index tests total.

title: Add PeriodIndex mean/median
description: Reality-check finding artifacts/api-coverage-drift-2026-05-08.md: PeriodIndex misses mean/median. Compute over ordinals; reject mixed-freq input. Single PR with regression tests.
close_reason: Shipped in 2dc1f3e: PeriodIndex now exposes pandas-parity mean/median over ordinals with mixed-freq rejection. 1 regression test passes; 343 fp-index tests total.

title: [coverage-drift] add DataFrame.rename column mapping alias
description: Live API coverage drift reports DataFrame.rename missing without an open child bead. The implementation already has DataFrame::rename_columns, rename_columns_map, rename_with, rename_index, and rename_index_with, but no pandas-named DataFrame::rename method. Add a narrow pandas-named DataFrame::rename(&[(&str, &str)]) wrapper over rename_columns for column mapping parity, preserve duplicate-column rejection through the existing implementation, and add focused fp-frame tests proving rename delegates to rename_columns, preserves unmapped columns/order, and rejects duplicate output names. Validate with targeted fp-frame tests through rch plus check/clippy/fmt and UBS on the touched file.
close_reason: Added DataFrame.rename column-mapping alias over rename_columns with delegation/order/duplicate-output tests; coverage drift no longer reports DataFrame.rename missing.

title: [modes-of-reasoning] DataFrame::to_csv renders whole-number Float64 as "3" instead of pandas-canonical "3.0"
description:
Skill: /modes-of-reasoning-project-analysis.

Discovered while pinning the to_csv_options golden in br-464b83:
fp-frame to_csv (lib.rs:25536) and to_csv_options (lib.rs:25614) render
Scalar::Float64(v) via v.to_string(). Rust f64::to_string strips trailing
.0 for whole numbers, so 3.0 -> "3" and pandas-roundtrip clients lose
the dtype hint.

Pandas is consistent:
  >>> pd.DataFrame({"a":[2.5, 3.0]}).to_csv(index=False)
  "a
2.5
3.0
"

The to_markdown (lib.rs:25857), to_latex (25987), and to_psql (26056)
paths in fp-frame ALREADY apply the right pattern:
  if *v == v.round() && v.abs() < 1e15 { format!("{v:.1}") } else { v.to_string() }
which produces 3.0 -> "3.0". The CSV paths just don't share that helper.

Fix: extract a small format_pandas_csv_float helper (same predicate as
to_markdown), call it from both CSV paths. Refresh the prior golden
(dataframe_to_csv_options_quoting_and_na_rep.txt) since 3.0 now renders
as "3.0".

title: [testing-golden-artifacts] DataFrame::to_csv_options has no golden lock-in for CSV escaping + na_rep
description:
Skill: /testing-golden-artifacts.

DataFrame::to_csv_options (lib.rs:25553) routes through csv_escape (lib.rs:901)
which double-quotes any string containing the separator, double-quote, or
newline/carriage-return, and escapes inner double-quotes by doubling them.
The function also substitutes na_rep for Null and Timedelta-NAT cells,
rounds bools to True/False, and supports a column subset via the columns
parameter.

Existing goldens cover only the default to_csv (no na_rep, no quoting test)
via dataframe_display.txt — nothing pins the to_csv_options surface.

A silent regression in csv_escape (e.g. forgetting to escape inner quotes,
swapping which characters trigger quoting, or breaking the na_rep insertion)
would compile and pass the unit tests but break consumers that round-trip
through pandas read_csv.

Fix: add dataframe_to_csv_options_quoting_and_na_rep.txt golden test
covering: a string with comma + double-quote + newline (forces escape),
Null cells replaced with na_rep "NA", boolean True/False, column subset.

title: [mock-code-finder] td_* family silently returns NAT/false for non-Timedelta operands instead of erroring
description:
Skill: /mock-code-finder.

fp-frame Series::td_* methods route through helpers (td_binary_op, td_scalar_op,
td_cmp_op, td_ratio at lib.rs:2224,2312,2337,2390) that all have catch-all
match arms:
  _ => Scalar::Timedelta64(NAT)        // td_binary_op, td_scalar_op
  _ => Scalar::Null(NullKind::NaN)      // td_ratio
  _ => Scalar::Bool(false)              // td_cmp_op

If a user calls e.g. td_add on a Float64 series (or mixed dtypes between
operands), the catch-all silently swallows the type mismatch and produces
all-NAT / all-false output instead of erroring.

Pandas raises TypeError for these ops on non-timedelta dtypes. Silent
all-false from td_gt is especially dangerous because it looks like the
comparison ran successfully.

Fix: add validate_timedelta_operand helper that accepts only DType::Timedelta64
or DType::Null (all-missing). Call it at the top of each helper before the
per-element loop. Also gate td_neg and td_abs which iterate directly.

title: Add Categorical/Period Index sort_values
description: Reality-check finding artifacts/api-coverage-drift-2026-05-08.md: CategoricalIndex/PeriodIndex miss sort_values. Both should sort labels ascending with appropriate semantics (PeriodIndex uses ordinal+freq guard; CategoricalIndex uses category position when ordered, lexicographic when unordered). Single PR with regression tests.
close_reason: Shipped in 3c4ab7e: PeriodIndex/CategoricalIndex now expose pandas-parity sort_values. 1 regression test passes; 312 fp-index tests total.

title: Add fp-io read_table tab-separated wrapper
description: Reality-check finding (artifacts/api-coverage-drift-2026-05-08.md): IOWrappers is missing read_table without open child bead. Pandas pd.read_table(path) is a thin wrapper around read_csv with sep='\t'. fp-io's CsvReadOptions already exposes a delimiter field. Add: (1) read_table(path) using delimiter b'\t' and CsvReadOptions defaults otherwise, (2) read_table_with_options(path, options) that swaps delimiter to b'\t' before delegating, (3) read_table_str(input) for in-memory parsing. Add regression tests covering basic tab-separated parsing, header inference, and missing-value handling parity with read_csv when delimiter is overridden. Single PR.
close_reason: Shipped in 4c30849: fp-io exposes read_table_str/read_table_with_options/read_table/read_table_with_options_path. Default delimiter is b'\t'; with-options variants honour explicit non-comma overrides. 4 regression tests pass; 517 fp-io tests total.

title: [deadlock-finder] subprocess pipe deadlock pattern in 6 fp-conformance test sites (sweep follow-up to br-92df89)
description:
Skill: /deadlock-finder-and-fixer.

br-92df89 fixed the synchronous-stdin-write-before-wait_with_output pattern
in capture_live_oracle_expected (lib.rs:12603). The same anti-pattern
exists in 6 conformance test sites:

  conformance_io.rs:187-198 (read_csv oracle)
  conformance_io_formats.rs (csv-formats oracle)
  conformance_groupby_apply.rs:129-145 (GroupBy.apply oracle)
  conformance_tseries.rs:154-170 (pandas function oracle, 1st)
  conformance_tseries.rs:285-301 (pandas offset oracle)
  conformance_tseries.rs:376+    (4th tseries oracle)

Each does:
  let mut stdin = child.stdin.take().ok_or_else(...)?;
  stdin.write_all(payload)?;
  drop(stdin);
  let output = child.wait_with_output()?;

For a payload exceeding the OS stdin pipe buffer (~64 KB) AND a child
that emits any output before reading all input filling its stdout pipe
buffer (~64 KB), parent and child deadlock on opposite-direction writes.

Test fixtures are mostly small but CSV roundtrip + scale fixtures can
hit it. Sweep all 6 sites with the same threaded-write fix.

title: [SeriesGroupBy] add grouper and level introspection
description: Implement a narrow SeriesGroupBy introspection surface under the SeriesGroupBy coverage umbrella. Required output: add SeriesGroupBy::grouper() and SeriesGroupBy::level() equivalents; grouper should expose the grouping Series name; level should report None because Series::groupby(by) does not yet support level-based grouping; add inline tests; validate with scoped fp-frame test/check/clippy/fmt plus UBS on touched Rust. Parent umbrella: frankenpandas-nt65g.
close_reason: Completed: added SeriesGroupBy grouper/level introspection with focused tests and scoped validation.

title: [testing-conformance-harnesses] 6 str-op python oracle handlers missing — 10+ live-oracle tests parked
description:
Skill: /testing-conformance-harnesses.

Same gap-shape as br-04aaef (find/rfind) and br-cfdaf3 (zfill). The
harness already has FixtureOperation::SeriesStr{Lower, Upper, Strip,
Len, Capitalize, Title} dispatchers on the Rust side, AND has 10+
pre-existing live_oracle_* tests (basic + unicode variants) targeting
those ops — but pandas_oracle.py has no handlers, so dispatch falls
through to OracleError(unsupported operation).

On the active-developer host (no legacy pandas), the tests skip via
OracleUnavailable and look green. On any host with legacy pandas
installed, they would fail with OracleCommandFailed instead of
running.

Tests waiting:
- live_oracle_series_str_lower_basic, _with_unicode
- live_oracle_series_str_upper_basic, _with_unicode
- live_oracle_series_str_len_basic, _with_unicode_and_empty
- live_oracle_series_str_strip_basic
- live_oracle_series_str_capitalize_basic, _with_unicode
- live_oracle_series_str_title_basic

Fix: add op_series_str_{lower,upper,strip,len,capitalize,title} handlers
that thinly delegate to series.str.<op>() (no parameters), wire each
into dispatch().

title: [testing-fuzzing] Timedelta::parse panics on Unicode fractional seconds
description: Fuzzing parser-boundary finding: Timedelta::parse handles colon time format fractional seconds with padded[..9], a byte-index slice. Non-ASCII fractional text such as 0:00:00.ééééé can panic on a non-character boundary instead of returning a typed InvalidFormat error. Fix by validating ASCII digits and building the nanosecond fraction without byte slicing; add regression coverage plus an oversized-hour overflow guard.
close_reason: Shipped fp-types Timedelta::parse Unicode-fraction panic fix; added regression for non-ASCII fractional seconds and oversized time overflow. rch fp-types tests/check/clippy/fmt passed; UBS exit 0.

title: [testing-metamorphic] No proptest for GroupBy::idxmin/idxmax round-trip invariant
close_reason: Shipped in 22c3d89: added GroupBy::idxmin/idxmax Utf8 round-trip proptests.

title: Add DatetimeIndex date/time accessors
description:
Reality-check finding (artifacts/api-coverage-drift-2026-05-08.md): DatetimeIndex still misses date/time accessors that decompose timestamps to chrono::NaiveDate/NaiveTime.

Add two methods on DatetimeIndex:
- date() -> Vec<Option<chrono::NaiveDate>> — extracts the calendar date part of each label; NAT propagates as None.
- time() -> Vec<Option<chrono::NaiveTime>> — extracts the within-day clock time of each label; NAT propagates as None.

Single PR. Add regression tests covering: known timestamp → matching NaiveDate (year-month-day) and NaiveTime (hh:mm:ss.nnnnnnnnn), midnight → 00:00:00.000000000 NaiveTime, NAT propagation.
close_reason: Shipped in aa1a80e: DatetimeIndex now exposes pandas-parity date/time accessors via map_datetime_labels + chrono::NaiveDate/NaiveTime. 1 regression test passes; 300 fp-index tests total.

title: [testing-conformance-harnesses] Live pandas oracle missing Series.str.removeprefix Unicode exactness
description: Fallback coverage finding after bv actionable rows were stale-closed in JSONL. fp-frame has unit coverage for Series.str.removeprefix Unicode normalization exactness, but fp-conformance lacked a live pandas oracle test proving composed U+00E9 and decomposed e+combining-acute behavior against the reference implementation. Add a strict live oracle case for series_str_removeprefix with prefix 'é' covering exact composed prefix removal, decomposed non-match, repeated composed prefix, and no-match baseline. Enable system pandas fallback so rch/CI hosts without legacy_pandas_code still execute the reference comparison instead of pass-by-skip.
close_reason: Added fp-conformance live-oracle regression test for Series.str.removeprefix Unicode exactness. Scoped rch cargo test passed; worker lacks pandas, so the test followed the existing oracle-unavailable skip path while still compiling and wiring the fixture.

title: Add Datetime/Timedelta Index get_loc and get_indexer
description: Reality-check finding artifacts/api-coverage-drift-2026-05-08.md: DatetimeIndex/TimedeltaIndex miss get_loc(value) and get_indexer(targets). Add get_loc as Result<usize, IndexError> with KeyError-style rejection, and get_indexer(&[value]) as Vec<isize> with -1 for missing. Single PR with regression tests.
close_reason: Shipped in f3ff6cd: Datetime/Timedelta Index now expose pandas-parity get_loc (KeyError-style on miss) and get_indexer (-1 for missing). 1 regression test passes; 310 fp-index tests total.

title: Add Datetime/Timedelta Index as_unit
description: Reality-check finding artifacts/api-coverage-drift-2026-05-08.md: typed Index variants miss as_unit. Storage is fixed at nanoseconds, so as_unit('ns') returns clone and other units reject with a typed compatibility error. Single PR with regression tests.
close_reason: Shipped in 4b591d6: Datetime/Timedelta Index now expose pandas-parity as_unit (ns no-op clone, others reject). 1 regression test passes; 329 fp-index tests total.

title: [profiling] select_columns O(|names|*|column_order|) position scan
close_reason: Shipped in 8ca2a9b: select_columns now uses a name-to-position map instead of repeated position scans.

title: [testing-metamorphic] No proptest for Series::shift inner-overlap-preservation invariant
description:
Skill: /testing-metamorphic.

fp-frame Series::shift (lib.rs:5110) inserts NaN at boundaries and
shifts the rest. Existing crate-level tests cover specific cases
(shift_basic, shift_negative, shift_zero, shift_with_nulls, etc.) but
no property test on random inputs.

Metamorphic invariant: for any Series s of length n and periods p
where -n < p < n:
  if p >= 0: shifted[i] == s[i - p] for i in [p, n)
  if p <  0: shifted[i] == s[i + |p|] for i in [0, n - |p|)
The rest of shifted is NaN.

Random inputs with mixed Int64/Float64/Bool/Null and varying length
will exercise corner cases the hand-written tests do not. A regression
that off-by-ones the boundary or scrambles the slice would surface.

title: Add PeriodIndex is_full accessor
description: Reality-check finding artifacts/api-coverage-drift-2026-05-08.md: PeriodIndex misses is_full. Returns true when ordinals are contiguous (no gaps) for the homogeneous-freq case. Single PR with regression tests.
close_reason: Shipped in 75794ea: PeriodIndex now exposes pandas-parity is_full (true for contiguous-ordinal homogeneous-freq inputs). 1 regression test passes; 328 fp-index tests total.

title: Add PeriodIndex intersection/union/difference/symmetric_difference
description: Reality-check finding artifacts/api-coverage-drift-2026-05-08.md: PeriodIndex still misses set-operation forwarders. Period derives Hash+Eq so all four ops can run via HashSet over Vec<Period>. Mixed-frequency input rejects via existing ensure_homogeneous_freq guard. Single PR with regression tests.
close_reason: Shipped in 932bf14: PeriodIndex now exposes pandas-parity intersection/union/difference/symmetric_difference with mixed-freq rejection and name reconciliation. 1 regression test passes; 314 fp-index tests total.

title: Add Datetime/Timedelta/Period/Range Index append/delete forwarders
description:
Reality-check finding (artifacts/api-coverage-drift-2026-05-08.md): the typed Index variants still miss append/delete. Mirrors the take/repeat/isin pattern from br-bbgg3.

Add two methods on each of DatetimeIndex / TimedeltaIndex / PeriodIndex / RangeIndex:
- append(other) -> Self — concatenates the underlying values; index name is preserved if both sides match (else dropped, matching pandas).
- delete(loc) -> Result<Self, IndexError> — removes the value at position loc; OOB raises IndexError::OutOfBounds.

RangeIndex.delete returns Index because the residual values may not form a contiguous range.

Single PR. Add regression tests covering: append concatenation + name reconciliation, delete removing the middle / first / last position, and OOB rejection.
close_reason: Shipped in 7cc4384: Datetime/Timedelta/Period/Range Index now expose pandas-parity append/delete with name reconciliation and OOB rejection. 4 regression tests pass; 285 fp-index tests total.

title: [modes-of-reasoning] cumprod proptest flaky on Int64 wrap vs Float64 precision
close_reason: Shipped in c76050c: fixed the cumprod scale proptest to avoid Int64 wrap and Float64 precision flake.

title: [testing-golden-artifacts] DataFrame.info and Series.info goldens are inline strings, not artifacts
description: Fallback golden-artifact finding after stale bv rows were compacted: fp-frame had DataFrame.info and Series.info tests named as goldens, but their expected text lived inline in src/lib.rs instead of checked-in golden files. That bypasses the existing assert_text_golden update/review workflow and makes formatter changes harder to inspect as artifacts. Move both deterministic info outputs to crates/fp-frame/tests/goldens and assert through assert_text_golden.
close_reason: Moved DataFrame.info and Series.info expected text into checked-in fp-frame golden files and switched tests to assert_text_golden. Scoped rch cargo test -p fp-frame info_golden passed.

title: Add RangeIndex prod
description: Reality-check finding artifacts/api-coverage-drift-2026-05-08.md: RangeIndex misses prod. Multiply all values in i128, narrow to i64. Empty returns 1. Single PR with regression test.
close_reason: Shipped in af69965: RangeIndex now exposes pandas-parity prod via i128 saturating accumulation. 1 regression test passes; 341 fp-index tests total.

title: [SeriesGroupBy] add pairwise corr/cov
description: Implement a narrow SeriesGroupBy pairwise statistics surface under the SeriesGroupBy coverage umbrella. Required output: add SeriesGroupBy::corr(other) and SeriesGroupBy::cov(other) returning one value per group, computed over aligned source-row pairs from the grouped Series and the supplied Series; preserve first-seen group order and group-key index labels; return NaN for groups with fewer than two valid numeric pairs or zero variance correlation; add inline tests for grouped covariance/correlation and singleton NaN; validate with scoped fp-frame test/check/clippy/fmt plus UBS on touched Rust. Parent umbrella: frankenpandas-nt65g.
close_reason: Implemented SeriesGroupBy pairwise corr/cov with focused validation.

title: [deadlock-finder] capture_live_oracle_expected can deadlock on >64KB payload due to synchronous stdin write before wait_with_output
description:
Skill: /deadlock-finder-and-fixer.

Pattern in fp-conformance/src/lib.rs:12603-12609 (capture_live_oracle_expected):
  .spawn()
  .and_then(|mut child| {
      if let Some(mut stdin) = child.stdin.take() {
          stdin.write_all(&input)?;     // <-- synchronous, blocks if pipe buffer fills
      }
      child.wait_with_output()           // <-- only here are stdout/stderr drained
  })?

Classic subprocess pipe deadlock:
1. input is JSON-serialized fixture payload — for wide DataFrames or
   long fixtures, can exceed the OS pipe buffer (~64 KB on Linux).
2. write_all blocks once the stdin pipe buffer fills, waiting for child
   to read more.
3. If the child has already started emitting output (stdout/stderr) and
   fills its own pipe buffer (also ~64 KB), the child blocks on write
   until parent drains it.
4. Parent has not started draining yet (still in step 2). Both sides
   are blocked on write -> deadlock.

Practical risk: capture_live_oracle_expected is the production path
called from every live_oracle_* test. Currently most fixtures are
small, but large dataframes (e.g., scale fixtures, get_dummies on
wide tables) will hit the deadlock.

Fix: spawn a thread to drain stdin (write input) concurrently with
the wait_with_output call. wait_with_output already uses internal
threads to drain stdout and stderr concurrently. Pattern is the
standard idiom for unknown-size piped IO.

title: Add Datetime/Timedelta Index slice_indexer returning std::ops::Range
description: Reality-check finding artifacts/api-coverage-drift-2026-05-08.md: typed Index variants miss slice_indexer. Returns std::ops::Range<usize> wrapping the slice_locs (start, end) result. Single PR with regression tests.
close_reason: Shipped in 86781d0: Datetime/Timedelta Index now expose pandas-parity slice_indexer returning std::ops::Range<usize>. 1 regression test passes; 332 fp-index tests total.

title: [coverage-drift] add DataFrame first/last offset aliases
description: Ready queue is empty while DataFrame API coverage drift still reports first/last missing. fp-frame already implements pandas-style DataFrame.first(offset) and DataFrame.last(offset) semantics as first_offset/last_offset. Add pandas-named DataFrame::first(&str) and DataFrame::last(&str) aliases that delegate to the existing offset implementations, preserving current DatetimeIndex selection behavior. Add focused fp-frame tests that the aliases match first_offset/last_offset and remove first/last from coverage drift. Validate with RCH targeted fp-frame tests plus fmt/check/clippy and UBS on the touched file.
close_reason: Added pandas-named DataFrame.first/last aliases over existing first_offset/last_offset with focused alias tests; coverage drift no longer reports first/last missing.

title: [porting-to-rust] Series::cov missing min_periods + ddof parameters
description:
Skill: /porting-to-rust.

pandas:
  pd.Series([1,2,3,4]).cov(other, min_periods=None, ddof=1)
  - ddof=0: population covariance (divisor n)
  - ddof=1: sample covariance (divisor n-1, default)
  - ddof=2: divisor n-2 (rarer)
  - min_periods: minimum non-null pair count required; otherwise NaN

fp-frame Series::cov(other) (lib.rs:8238) -> Series::cov_with(other) ->
cov_components hardcodes (n - 1.0) at lib.rs:8276 and has no min_periods
threshold (only the implicit count < 2 -> NaN check).

DataFrame::corr already exposes min_periods (corr_min_periods at line 23601);
the Series-level cov surface is missing the equivalent.

Port: add Series::cov_with_options(other, min_periods, ddof) -> Result<f64>.
Validate ddof < count to avoid divide-by-zero. Apply min_periods threshold.
Series::cov_with(other) becomes a thin wrapper passing default ddof=1, no
min_periods. Existing cov_components keeps its computation; add a thin
ddof-aware wrapper for the divisor.

title: [testing-conformance-harnesses] 5 more str-op oracle handlers missing
close_reason: Shipped in 11df96f: added live pandas oracle handlers for five more string operations.

title: [MultiIndex] add from_frame constructor parity
description: Implement the narrow pandas MultiIndex.from_frame constructor surface under the MultiIndex parity umbrella. Because fp-index cannot depend on fp-frame, expose a payload-level MultiIndex::from_frame(columns: Vec<(Option<String>, Vec<IndexLabel>)>) that takes frame column names plus column values in order, validates equal column lengths, builds row tuples, and preserves names. Add focused inline tests for named columns, empty columns, and length mismatch; validate fp-index fmt/test/check/clippy plus UBS on touched Rust. Parent umbrella: frankenpandas-d89fe.
close_reason: Completed: added payload-level MultiIndex::from_frame constructor preserving names and validating equal column lengths, with focused coverage.

title: Add RangeIndex isna/notna/hasnans/dropna/fillna/format/factorize closed-form methods
description:
Reality-check finding (artifacts/api-coverage-drift-2026-05-08.md): IndexVariants RangeIndex is missing the standard missing-data accessors and format/factorize entry points. RangeIndex is int64-typed and uniqueness-by-construction, so each method is a closed-form expression with no per-position scanning.

Add seven inherent methods on RangeIndex:
- isna() -> Vec<bool> — vec![false; len]
- isnull() -> Vec<bool> — alias for isna
- notna() -> Vec<bool> — vec![true; len]
- notnull() -> Vec<bool> — alias for notna
- hasnans() -> bool — false
- dropna() -> Self — self.clone()
- fillna(_value: i64) -> Self — self.clone()
- format() -> Vec<String> — values stringified
- factorize() -> (Vec<usize>, Self) — identity codes [0..len], uniques == self

Mirrors the existing closed-form pattern from br-frankenpandas-mrchb (argmax/argmin/argsort/duplicated/drop_duplicates). Add regression tests covering empty range, ascending range, and descending range. Single PR.
close_reason: Shipped in 005432f: RangeIndex now exposes pandas-parity isna/isnull/notna/notnull/hasnans/dropna/fillna/format/factorize as closed-form constants and identity factorization. 3 regression tests pass; 255 fp-index tests total.

title: [porting-to-rust] Series::quantile missing interpolation parameter (lower/higher/nearest/midpoint)
description:
Skill: /porting-to-rust.

pandas:
  pd.Series([1,2,3,4]).quantile(0.5, interpolation=...)
  - linear   -> 2.5  (default; weighted mean of neighbors)
  - lower    -> 2    (lower neighbor)
  - higher   -> 3    (upper neighbor)
  - nearest  -> 2    (closest neighbor; ties go to lower)
  - midpoint -> 2.5  (midpoint of neighbors)

fp-frame Series::quantile(q) (lib.rs:4274) only implements linear via
percentile_linear_series helper — no interpolation parameter at all.
Same pandas API gap exists for the broader DataFrame::quantile and
GroupBy::quantile but the Series surface is the foundational one.

Port: add Series::quantile_with_interpolation(q, interpolation) returning
Result<Scalar, FrameError>. Validate interpolation up front. Reuse the
existing sort + neighbor-position logic; switch the interpolation kernel.

title: Add Datetime/Timedelta Index fillna/notnull/isnull/hasnans
description:
Reality-check finding (artifacts/api-coverage-drift-2026-05-08.md): the typed Index variants miss the explicit fillna entry plus pandas-style isnull/notnull aliases for the existing isna/notna helpers.

Add four methods on each of DatetimeIndex / TimedeltaIndex:
- fillna(value) -> Self — replace NAT positions with value (i64 ns); preserves the index name.
- isnull() -> Vec<bool> — alias for isna.
- notnull() -> Vec<bool> — alias for notna.

Single PR. Add regression tests covering: fillna replaces NAT only, name preservation, isnull/notnull alias coverage.
close_reason: Shipped in 084b5c6: Datetime/Timedelta Index now expose pandas-parity fillna/isnull/notnull. 1 regression test passes; 302 fp-index tests total.

title: [profiling] mode_values O(n²) Vec::iter_mut().find — build ModeKey to enable HashMap bucketing across cross-dtype numeric equivalence
description:
Skill: /profiling-software-performance.

mode_values (fp-frame/src/lib.rs:711) is the cross-dtype mode helper used
by DataFrame::mode_with_options axis=1 (line 27539). Each row may contain
heterogeneous Scalar variants. The bucket key uses pandas-style numeric
equivalence (grouped_scalar_eq, line 517):
  Bool(true) == Int64(1) == Float64(1.0)
  Bool(false) == Int64(0) == Float64(0.0)
  Float64(f) == Int64(n)  iff f == n as f64

The current impl walks counts: Vec<(Scalar, usize)> via iter_mut().find()
on every input — O(n × m) where m = distinct count. For wide DataFrames
with many rows, this becomes the rate-limiting step in axis=1 mode.

Fix: define a ModeKey enum that normalizes the equivalence classes:
  Int(i64): Bool, Int64, Float64-whole-finite-in-i64-range
  NonIntFloat(u64): Float64 otherwise (via to_bits)
  Utf8(&str), Timedelta(i64), Null, NaN
Use HashMap<ModeKey, usize> for index lookup. O(n) amortized.

The Series-level mode_with_dropna (line 5143) already uses ScalarKey-based
HashMap (br-cec8f) since Series has homogeneous columns. This fix brings
the same speedup to the cross-dtype helper.

title: Add PeriodIndex::from_ordinals constructor
description: Reality-check finding artifacts/api-coverage-drift-2026-05-08.md: PeriodIndex misses from_ordinals constructor. Build PeriodIndex from raw ordinal values and a PeriodFreq. Single PR with regression test.
close_reason: Shipped in ddeb3ee: PeriodIndex now exposes pandas-parity from_ordinals constructor. 1 regression test passes; 330 fp-index tests total.

title: Add Datetime/Timedelta/Period/Range Index take/repeat/isin forwarders
description:
Reality-check finding (artifacts/api-coverage-drift-2026-05-08.md): the typed Index variants still miss take/repeat/isin. Mirrors the forwarder pattern from br-z9guv/vq4pf/i1q1c/zke9k, restricted to operations that don't require monotonic ordering.

Add three methods on each of DatetimeIndex / TimedeltaIndex / PeriodIndex / RangeIndex:
- take(positions) -> Result<Self, IndexError> — clones the underlying values at each given position; out-of-bounds positions return IndexError::OutOfBounds.
- repeat(repeats) -> Self — repeats each value 'repeats' times.
- isin(values) -> Vec<bool> — per-position membership mask against a slice of the variant's value type (i64 nanos / i64 nanos / Period / i64 ints).

Single PR. Add regression tests covering: a 3-element index where take swaps positions, repeat doubles each value, isin matches a subset, plus out-of-bounds rejection.
close_reason: Shipped in e3a04f0: Datetime/Timedelta/Period/Range Index now expose pandas-parity take/repeat/isin with bound-checking and OOB rejection. 4 regression tests pass; 281 fp-index tests total.

title: [deadlock-finder] recover_once unbounded loop under buggy RecoveryPolicy
close_reason: Shipped in 454b3b6: bounded recover_once retry loop at max_attempts even under buggy RecoveryPolicy.

title: Add CategoricalIndex isnull/notnull/hasnans/fillna/dropna
description: Reality-check finding artifacts/api-coverage-drift-2026-05-08.md: CategoricalIndex misses missing-data accessors. Labels are always non-null in our model, so all five methods are trivially closed-form. Single PR with regression tests.
close_reason: Shipped in 6015f9d: CategoricalIndex now exposes pandas-parity isnull/notnull/hasnans/dropna/fillna as closed-form non-null defaults. 1 regression test passes; 316 fp-index tests total.

title: [testing-fuzzing] No property/fuzz coverage for Series::str.find/rfind char-position invariant on random multi-byte UTF-8
description:
Skill: /testing-fuzzing.

br-frankenpandas-02ae2b fixed Series::str.find/rfind to return char positions
matching pandas. Existing coverage:
- 6 hand-coded regression tests in fp-frame (héllo, café_lo, héllé, etc.)
- 2 live-pandas-oracle tests in fp-conformance (live_oracle_series_str_find_*)

Both use small, fixed inputs. Random multi-byte UTF-8 inputs (combining marks,
4-byte emoji, mixed scripts) could expose corner cases not covered by the
hand-picked test set. The metamorphic invariant is universal:
  if find(sub, s) returns p (>= 0), then chars(s).skip(p).take(|sub|) == sub

Fix: add proptest that generates random multi-byte UTF-8 haystacks and char-aligned
substrings, asserts the char-position invariant for both find() and rfind().
Locks in pandas parity for any future refactor of these methods.

title: Add MultiIndex diff/round/astype reject-closed surfaces
description: Reality-check finding (artifacts/api-coverage-drift-2026-05-08.md): MultiIndex is missing diff/round/astype methods. Pandas 2.2.3 raises TypeError/NotImplementedError on each because hierarchical indices can't support these scalar/numeric semantics. Mirror the existing reject-closed pattern used by MultiIndex::shift, MultiIndex::r#str, MultiIndex::asof — add typed methods that return IndexError::InvalidArgument with pandas-style messages, plus regression tests asserting the exact message shape. Single PR.
close_reason: Shipped in 426fcfa: MultiIndex now exposes pandas-parity diff/round/astype methods. astype('object'/'O') clones, astype('category') rejects with NotImplementedError-style message, other dtypes reject with TypeError-style message. diff/round are reject-closed (tuple subtraction / ufunc rint not supported). 3 regression tests pass in fp-index (248 total).

title: Add Datetime/Timedelta Index unit/resolution accessors
description: Reality-check finding artifacts/api-coverage-drift-2026-05-08.md: DatetimeIndex/TimedeltaIndex miss unit/resolution. FrankenPandas's storage is fixed-resolution nanoseconds so both return 'ns'. Single PR with regression tests.
close_reason: Shipped in 6331deb: Datetime/Timedelta Index now expose pandas-parity unit/resolution constants (ns/nanosecond). 1 regression test passes; 309 fp-index tests total.

title: [porting-to-rust] Series::factorize missing sort + use_na_sentinel options (pandas-2.2 surface)
description:
Skill: /porting-to-rust.

pandas:
  pd.Series([...]).factorize(sort=False, use_na_sentinel=True)

fp-frame Series::factorize() takes no parameters — exposes only the
sort=False/use_na_sentinel=True default. fp-columnar already implements
Column::factorize_with_options(sort, use_na_sentinel) — see br-9433f.
The fp-frame Series wrapper just needs a thin pass-through and Series
reconstruction.

Port:
  pub fn factorize_with_options(&self, sort: bool, use_na_sentinel: bool)
      -> Result<(Series, Series), FrameError>

Behavior:
  - sort=true: uniques are sorted; codes remapped to new positions.
  - use_na_sentinel=false: missing values become a regular bucket with
    their own code instead of -1.

title: [coverage-drift] add typed IndexVariant to_frame/to_series forwarders
description: Fallback coverage-drift slice after ready queue emptied with fp-frame already claimed. Current API coverage drift reports IndexVariants missing pandas to_frame/to_series while base Index and MultiIndex already expose bounded materialization helpers. Add typed DatetimeIndex, TimedeltaIndex, PeriodIndex, RangeIndex, and CategoricalIndex to_frame()/to_series() forwarders via their existing flat Index representation, preserving label order and name through the existing to_index/to_flat_index paths. Add focused fp-index tests for representative typed labels, then validate with RCH fmt/test/check/clippy plus UBS on the touched file.
close_reason: Added typed IndexVariant to_frame/to_series forwarders through existing flat-index materialization for DatetimeIndex, TimedeltaIndex, PeriodIndex, RangeIndex, and CategoricalIndex; extended fp-index materialization tests and verified coverage drift no longer lists to_frame/to_series as missing.

title: [testing-conformance-harnesses] Live pandas oracle missing op_series_str_zfill — sign-handling parity not pinned
description:
Skill: /testing-conformance-harnesses.

fp-frame Series::str.zfill (lib.rs:13963) handles the leading +/- sign
specially when zero-padding (matches Python str.zfill semantics):
  pd.Series(["5","-5","+5"]).str.zfill(4) -> ["0005","-005","+005"]

The conformance harness already exposes FixtureOperation::SeriesStrZfill
(lib.rs:650, 15925) reading fixture.str_width, but pandas_oracle.py has
no op_series_str_zfill handler. dispatch() falls through to OracleError(
unsupported operation), so any live-oracle test on zfill would error.

Effect: sign-handling pandas-parity is verified only by hand-coded crate
tests; no live-oracle regression net for the (sign-prefix, multi-byte
UTF-8, non-numeric inputs) edge cases.

Fix:
1. Add op_series_str_zfill(pd, payload) to pandas_oracle.py
2. Wire into dispatch()
3. Add live_oracle_series_str_zfill_sign_handling test in fp-conformance
   covering signed numerics, multi-byte UTF-8 ("5é"), and ASCII-only
   inputs across width=0/3/8.

title: Add CategoricalIndex searchsorted/intersection/union/difference
description: Reality-check finding artifacts/api-coverage-drift-2026-05-08.md: CategoricalIndex still misses searchsorted plus the four set-operations. Forward via to_index() to the underlying Index. Single PR with regression tests.
close_reason: Shipped in 51bd25e: CategoricalIndex now exposes pandas-parity searchsorted + 3 set ops (intersection/union/difference). 1 regression test passes; 313 fp-index tests total.

title: Add Index variants view/transpose/ravel/nlevels/infer_objects
description:
Reality-check finding (artifacts/api-coverage-drift-2026-05-08.md): the typed Index variants still miss the 'identity-like' surface that pandas exposes on every Index. Mirrors the forwarder pattern from earlier beads.

Add five methods on each of DatetimeIndex / TimedeltaIndex / PeriodIndex / RangeIndex / CategoricalIndex:
- view() -> Self — clones self (pandas returns a view; FrankenPandas's owned-storage model materializes a fresh clone).
- transpose() -> Self — 1D transpose is identity, matching pandas.
- ravel() -> the variant's value type vector (Vec<Option<i64>> for Datetime/Timedelta, Vec<Period> for PeriodIndex, Vec<i64> for RangeIndex, Vec<String> for CategoricalIndex).
- nlevels() -> usize — always 1 for a flat Index variant (pandas returns 1).
- infer_objects() -> Self — no-op for typed indexes (pandas re-infers dtypes; typed indexes stay typed).

Single PR. Add regression tests covering: identity behavior of view/transpose/infer_objects, ravel returning the same values as values(), and nlevels==1.
close_reason: Shipped in ee49689: 5 Index variants now expose pandas-parity view/transpose/ravel/nlevels/infer_objects. 1 regression test exercises the matrix; 290 fp-index tests total.

title: [mock-code-finder] DatetimeAccessor silently returns NaN for non-Utf8 series instead of erroring (pandas raises AttributeError)
description:
pandas:
  >>> pd.Series([1.0, 2.0]).dt.year
  AttributeError: Can only use .dt accessor with datetimelike values

fp-frame DatetimeAccessor::extract_component (crates/fp-frame/src/lib.rs:14782) and
try_extract_component (crates/fp-frame/src/lib.rs:14799) have catch-all match arms
"_ => Scalar::Null(NullKind::NaN)" that silently swallow Float64/Int64/Bool/Timedelta64
series, returning all-NaN. This hides type errors from users.

Skill: /mock-code-finder — silent-stub catch-all in match.

Fix: validate dtype at start of helpers; allow only Utf8 (fp-frame stores datetime as ISO
string) and Null (all-missing). Return CompatibilityRejected for other dtypes.
Affects all .dt accessor methods: year, month, day, hour, minute, second,
quarter, weekday, day_of_year, etc.

title: Add CategoricalIndex argmax/argmin
description: Reality-check finding artifacts/api-coverage-drift-2026-05-08.md: CategoricalIndex misses argmax/argmin. ordered=true uses category position; unordered uses lexicographic ordering. Empty rejects pandas-style. Single PR with regression tests.
close_reason: Shipped in 73138b0: CategoricalIndex now exposes pandas-parity argmax/argmin with ordered/unordered ordering distinction. 1 regression test passes; 344 fp-index tests total.

title: [testing-fuzzing] No proptest for Series::quantile_with_interpolation cross-mode ordering invariant
description:
Skill: /testing-fuzzing.

br-frankenpandas-a56003 added Series::quantile_with_interpolation with 5
modes (linear/lower/higher/nearest/midpoint). 14 hand-coded tests cover
fixed inputs but no random property test asserts the cross-mode ordering
invariant.

Strong invariants on any non-empty numeric Series s and q in [0,1]:
  quantile(q, lower)   <= quantile(q, nearest) <= quantile(q, higher)
  quantile(q, lower)   <= quantile(q, linear)  <= quantile(q, higher)
  quantile(q, lower)   <= quantile(q, midpoint) <= quantile(q, higher)
  quantile(q, midpoint) == (quantile(q, lower) + quantile(q, higher)) / 2

Random Series and q surface boundary cases the hand-written tests miss
(small Series of length 2, q very close to 0 or 1, identical-value runs).

title: [coverage-drift] add typed IndexVariant sort aliases
description: Fallback coverage-drift slice after ready queue emptied with fp-frame already claimed. Current API coverage drift reports IndexVariants missing pandas sort even though base Index/MultiIndex expose sort() and typed variants already expose sort_values(). Add pandas-style sort() aliases on DatetimeIndex, TimedeltaIndex, PeriodIndex, RangeIndex, and CategoricalIndex delegating to sort_values(), preserving existing result/error semantics. Add focused fp-index tests proving alias equivalence and run RCH fmt/test/check/clippy plus UBS on the touched file.
close_reason: Added pandas-style sort aliases on DatetimeIndex, TimedeltaIndex, PeriodIndex, RangeIndex, and CategoricalIndex that delegate to existing sort_values() implementations while preserving result/error semantics. Extended existing fp-index sort tests to assert alias equivalence and verified coverage drift no longer lists sort as missing for IndexVariants.

title: [profiling] DataFrame::normalize_column_order O(n²) dedup-keep-last in DataFrame constructor hot path
description:
Skill: /profiling-software-performance.

crates/fp-frame/src/lib.rs:18374 normalize_column_order is called from
DataFrame::new_with_column_order (line 18682) — i.e., every DataFrame
construction with explicit ordering. Three quadratic ops:
  1. normalized.iter().position(|entry| entry == &name)  -- O(n) per name
  2. normalized.remove(existing_idx)                      -- O(n) shift
  3. normalized.iter().any(|entry| entry == name)         -- O(n) per missing column

For column_order of size N with duplicates, total O(N²). Becomes a real
bottleneck in get_dummies / transpose / pivot / wide-DataFrame paths that
produce hundreds of columns.

Fix: pre-compute last_idx HashMap<name, usize> once, then iterate forward
emitting only at the last-occurrence index. Use HashSet<&str> for the
missing-column check. O(n+k) total where n = column_order.len(), k = columns.len().

title: Add Datetime/Timedelta Index to_pydatetime/to_pytimedelta/to_julian_date
description:
Reality-check finding (artifacts/api-coverage-drift-2026-05-08.md): the typed Index variants miss the Python-conversion accessors.

Add three methods:
- DatetimeIndex.to_pydatetime() -> Vec<Option<chrono::DateTime<chrono::Utc>>> — chrono::DateTime values; NAT collapses to None.
- TimedeltaIndex.to_pytimedelta() -> Vec<Option<chrono::Duration>> — chrono::Duration values; NAT collapses to None.
- DatetimeIndex.to_julian_date() -> Vec<Option<f64>> — Julian Date numbers via the standard formula JD = (unix_seconds / 86400) + 2440587.5.

Single PR. Add regression tests covering: known datetime → matching chrono::DateTime + Julian-Date value, NAT propagation, and TimedeltaIndex → chrono::Duration + NAT.
close_reason: Shipped in 33cbe62: DatetimeIndex now exposes pandas-parity to_pydatetime + to_julian_date; TimedeltaIndex exposes to_pytimedelta. 2 regression tests pass; 299 fp-index tests total.

title: [modes-of-reasoning] prop_dataframe_abs_is_sign_flip_invariant fails on Int64 inputs due to mul_scalar dtype promotion
description:
Skill: /modes-of-reasoning-project-analysis.

Failing proptest: prop_dataframe_abs_is_sign_flip_invariant.
Minimal failing input: df = {a: [Int64(0)], b: [Int64(0)]}.

Property: df.abs().equals(df.mul_scalar(-1.0).abs()).

Root cause analysis:
1. df has Int64 columns (a=0, b=0).
2. baseline = df.abs() preserves Int64 dtype: {a: Int64(0), b: Int64(0)}.
3. flipped = df.mul_scalar(-1.0). apply_scalar_op (lib.rs:29283) ALWAYS
   emits Scalar::Float64 line 29299, so flipped has Float64 dtype.
4. flipped_abs = flipped.abs() = {a: Float64(0.0), b: Float64(0.0)}.
5. baseline.equals(flipped_abs) -> false (dtype mismatch — equals is
   dtype-strict, matching pandas .equals).

This is a test-design bug, not an fp-frame bug. pandas itself promotes
Int64 * float -> Float64 (>>> (pd.DataFrame({"a":[0]}) * -1.0).dtypes
== a:float64), and pandas .equals() is also dtype-strict.

Fix: replace baseline.equals(&flipped_abs) with the existing
approx_equal_dataframe helper, which converts both sides to f64 with
tolerance — dtype-agnostic. Same shape as 14 other proptests in the
file that use approx_equal_* helpers when arithmetic is involved.

title: Add CategoricalIndex argsort/take/isin/get_loc/min/max
description: Reality-check finding artifacts/api-coverage-drift-2026-05-08.md: CategoricalIndex still misses common forwarders. Add argsort/take/isin/get_loc/min/max via to_index forwarding. Single PR with regression tests.
close_reason: Shipped in d67c67a: CategoricalIndex now exposes 6 pandas-parity forwarders (argsort/take/isin/get_loc/min/max) with ordered/unordered min/max distinction. 1 regression test passes; 311 fp-index tests total.

title: [mock-code-finder] Series::cumsum/cummin/cummax errors on Utf8+Null mix instead of skipna pass-through
description:
Skill: /mock-code-finder.

Series::cumsum (lib.rs:5288), cummin (lib.rs:5384), and cummax (lib.rs:5446)
each have a Utf8 specialization gated by:
  values.iter().all(|v| matches!(v, Scalar::Utf8(_)))

Any Null in the series breaks the homogeneity check, so cumsum/cummin/cummax
on a Utf8 series with missing values falls through to the float64 path,
which then errors at val.to_f64() because Scalar::Utf8("a") cannot parse
as f64.

Pandas (skipna=True default) preserves Null positions and updates the
accumulator only on non-missing values:
  >>> pd.Series(["a", None, "b"]).cummax()
  0      a
  1    NaN
  2      b

Fix: loosen the gate to accept Utf8 OR missing, with at least one Utf8.
In the loop, branch on missing (push NaN, skip acc update) vs Utf8
(update acc, push the running result).

Affects:
- Series::cumsum (lib.rs:5288)
- Series::cummin (lib.rs:5384)
- Series::cummax (lib.rs:5446)

title: [profiling] Series::between dispatches inclusive string match per element instead of once per call
description:
Skill: /profiling-software-performance.

fp-frame Series::between (lib.rs:5593) validates inclusive string at the
top, then re-matches the same string for every value in the closure body:
  match inclusive {
      "both" => ...,
      "neither" => ...,
      "left" => ...,
      "right" => ...,
      _ => unreachable!(),
  }

For a Series of N elements, this runs N str-equality dispatches when the
mode is fixed for the entire call. Each is short but constant work
multiplied across the loop adds up on filtering hot paths.

Fix: define a small enum InclusiveMode { Both, Neither, Left, Right };
resolve  to a mode once before the loop; match the enum
(jump-table) inside the closure. Same micro-opt shape as br-78d0c
(Vec::iter().position to HashMap) but here it is dispatch hoisting.

description:
Reality-check finding (artifacts/api-coverage-drift-2026-05-08.md): DatetimeIndex still missing locale-stable string accessors and the normalize/is_normalized pair. Mirrors the day-of-X (br-k860x) and boundary (br-qy7yd) patterns.

Add four pandas-named methods on DatetimeIndex:
- month_name() -> Vec<Option<String>> — full English month name (January..December). Pandas defaults to None locale (English).
- day_name() -> Vec<Option<String>> — full English weekday name (Monday..Sunday).
- normalize() -> Self — return a new DatetimeIndex with each timestamp truncated to midnight UTC (subsecond + time-of-day zeroed).
- is_normalized -> bool — true when every label is at 00:00:00.000000000 (or NAT).

Single PR. Add regression tests covering January/December name formatting, Monday/Sunday name formatting, normalize zeroing time-of-day while preserving the date and NAT, and is_normalized returning false for non-midnight, true for midnight, and true for empty/all-NAT.

title: Add Period/Range Index min/max + Range Index sum/mean closed-form reductions
description:
Reality-check finding (artifacts/api-coverage-drift-2026-05-08.md): PeriodIndex/RangeIndex still miss the basic ordering / arithmetic reductions.

Add four methods:
- PeriodIndex.min() -> Result<Option<Period>, IndexError> — minimum period by ordinal; rejects on mixed-frequency input.
- PeriodIndex.max() -> Result<Option<Period>, IndexError> — maximum.
- RangeIndex.min() / max() -> Option<i64> — closed form on (start, stop, step, len). Empty returns None. Negative step flips the end choice.
- RangeIndex.sum() / mean() -> closed-form via arithmetic-progression formula n * (first + last) / 2; mean returns Option<f64>.

Single PR. Add regression tests covering: ascending Period min/max, mixed-freq rejection, RangeIndex with positive step / negative step / empty, and arithmetic-progression sum/mean for known cases (e.g. 1..=10 step 1 sum=55, mean=5.5).
close_reason: Shipped in 68c47b4: PeriodIndex now exposes pandas-parity min/max with mixed-freq rejection; RangeIndex now exposes pandas-parity min/max/sum/mean via O(1) closed-form arithmetic. 2 regression tests pass; 295 fp-index tests total.

title: Add CategoricalIndex unique/duplicated/drop_duplicates/value_counts/factorize
description:
Reality-check finding (artifacts/api-coverage-drift-2026-05-08.md): CategoricalIndex still misses the dedup / value-count / factorize surface. Forward through the existing to_index() bridge that materializes labels as a Utf8 Index, then rebuild as CategoricalIndex via with_categories() so the categorical metadata is preserved when needed.

Add five methods on CategoricalIndex:
- unique() -> Self — first-seen unique labels using HashSet, preserves the categories list and ordered flag.
- duplicated(keep) -> Vec<bool> — forwarded to Index::duplicated through to_index().
- drop_duplicates() -> Self — first-seen filter; categories preserved.
- value_counts() -> Vec<(String, usize)> — pandas drops NaN by default; CategoricalIndex labels are non-null, so total == len.
- factorize() -> (Vec<isize>, Self) — standard first-seen factorization; uniques is a CategoricalIndex.

Single PR. Add regression tests covering: a 6-label index with duplicates, dedup ordering preservation, factorize codes round-trip, and value_counts total.

title: Add Datetime/Timedelta Index intersection/union/difference/symmetric_difference
description:
Reality-check finding (artifacts/api-coverage-drift-2026-05-08.md): DatetimeIndex/TimedeltaIndex still miss the four set-operation forwarders. Mirrors the typed forwarder pattern from br-bbgg3 / br-834v9.

Add four methods on each of DatetimeIndex / TimedeltaIndex:
- intersection(other) -> Self — labels in both, first-seen order.
- union(other) -> Self — labels from self followed by labels from other not already present.
- difference(other) -> Self — labels in self not in other.
- symmetric_difference(other) -> Self — labels in either but not both, preserving self-then-other order.

Each preserves the index name when both operands share it (otherwise dropped, matching pandas).

Single PR. Add regression tests covering: typical 3-element vs 3-element overlap, empty operand passthrough, NAT membership semantics (NAT == NAT), and name reconciliation.
close_reason: Shipped in 0fe2252: Datetime/Timedelta Index now expose pandas-parity intersection/union/difference/symmetric_difference with first-seen ordering, deduplication, and name reconciliation. 2 regression tests pass; 289 fp-index tests total.

title: Add DatetimeIndex day-of-X and quarter/leap accessors
description:
Reality-check finding (artifacts/api-coverage-drift-2026-05-08.md): IndexVariants is missing the broader DatetimeIndex date accessors. Mirrors the existing year/month/day pattern (chrono::Datelike via map_datetime_labels) and the new hour/minute/second time-of-day pattern (br-znejf).

Add eight pandas-named methods:
- dayofyear / day_of_year -> Vec<Option<u32>> via chrono::Datelike::ordinal (1..=366).
- dayofweek / day_of_week / weekday -> Vec<Option<u32>> via Weekday::num_days_from_monday (0..=6, Monday=0).
- quarter -> Vec<Option<u32>> via (month - 1) / 3 + 1 (1..=4).
- is_leap_year -> Vec<Option<bool>> via chrono::Datelike::leap_year().
- days_in_month / daysinmonth -> Vec<Option<u32>> via num_days_in_month(year, month) helper (28/29/30/31).

Single PR. Add regression tests covering ordinal day, weekday alignment to pandas Mon=0..Sun=6, quarter at month boundaries, is_leap_year for 2000 (true) / 2024 (true) / 2100 (false) / 2023 (false), days_in_month for Feb 2024 (29) / Feb 2023 (28) / Apr (30) / Dec (31), plus NAT propagation.
close_reason: Shipped in 6b44c46: DatetimeIndex now exposes pandas-parity dayofyear/day_of_year/dayofweek/day_of_week/weekday/quarter/is_leap_year/days_in_month/daysinmonth via map_datetime_labels + chrono::Datelike + days_in_calendar_month helper. 2 regression tests pass; 259 fp-index tests total.

description:
Reality-check finding (artifacts/api-coverage-drift-2026-05-08.md): DatetimeIndex and TimedeltaIndex still miss the basic ordering reductions and sort surface. Mirrors br-bbgg3/834v9 pattern.

Add three methods on each:
- min() -> Option<i64> — minimum non-NAT label (pandas returns NaT/None for all-NAT or empty).
- max() -> Option<i64> — maximum non-NAT label.
- sort_values() -> Self — ascending sort. NAT sorts first because i64::MIN/Timedelta::NAT < every other value (matches pandas with na_position='first', the default for datetime indexes).

Single PR. Add regression tests covering: min/max on a 4-element index with NAT, all-NAT returns None, and sort_values on duplicated input.

title: Add RangeIndex argmax/argmin/argsort/duplicated/drop_duplicates
description:
Reality-check finding (artifacts/api-coverage-drift-2026-05-08.md): IndexVariants is missing 134 pandas methods without open child beads. RangeIndex's argmax/argmin/argsort/duplicated/drop_duplicates are five of the simplest gaps because RangeIndex is uniqueness-by-construction and monotonic — every result is a closed-form expression of (start, stop, step, len).

Add five inherent methods on RangeIndex:
- argmax() -> Result<usize, IndexError> — len-1 when step>0; 0 when step<0; error on empty.
- argmin() -> Result<usize, IndexError> — mirror of argmax.
- argsort() -> Vec<usize> — [0..len) when step>0; [len-1..=0] when step<0.
- duplicated(keep: DuplicateKeep) -> Vec<bool> — always vec![false; len] (RangeIndex enforces uniqueness).
- drop_duplicates() -> Self — clones self because RangeIndex never has duplicates.

Add regression tests covering ascending step, descending step, empty index, and large step values. Single PR. Mirrors existing pattern on base Index (lines 1779/1849 etc. for round/diff).
close_reason: Shipped in e61c4fd: RangeIndex now exposes pandas-parity argmax/argmin/argsort/duplicated/drop_duplicates with O(1) closed-form implementations and pandas-style empty-sequence rejection. 4 regression tests pass; 252 fp-index tests total.

title: [coverage-drift] normalize Rust raw identifiers in API scanner
description: Live API coverage drift falsely reports pandas methods implemented with Rust raw identifiers as missing. Concrete case: DataFrame.mod is implemented as Rust raw identifier r#mod in crates/fp-frame/src/lib.rs with existing tests, but scripts/validate_api_coverage_drift.py records the method name as r#mod, so target method mod remains in missing_without_open_child. Fix the scanner Rust method extraction to normalize raw identifiers by stripping the r# prefix for impl, trait impl, and top-level functions. Add or update validation so DataFrame.mod is counted as implemented and no longer appears as a missing untracked DataFrame method. Validate with the coverage drift script and a focused script-level check.

title: Add Datetime/Timedelta Index where/putmask
description:
Reality-check finding (artifacts/api-coverage-drift-2026-05-08.md): DatetimeIndex/TimedeltaIndex still miss the mask-based where/putmask surface that flat Index already exposes (lines 5103/5131 for MultiIndex equivalents, plus the Index implementations).

Add two methods on each:
- where(cond, other) -> Result<Self, IndexError> — positions with cond=false get replaced with other; cond and self must have equal length.
- putmask(mask, value) -> Result<Self, IndexError> — positions with mask=true get replaced with value; mask and self must have equal length.

Both functions take the value as the variant's native type (i64 nanos for Datetime/Timedelta) so callers can pass i64::MIN / Timedelta::NAT for NAT replacement.

Single PR. Add regression tests covering: where with mixed cond replacing some positions, putmask replacing all true positions, length-mismatch rejection, and NAT-as-other replacement.
close_reason: Shipped in 061d0f0: Datetime/Timedelta Index now expose pandas-parity where/putmask with length-mismatch rejection. 2 regression tests pass; 293 fp-index tests total.

title: Add Datetime/Timedelta Index var reduction
description: Reality-check finding artifacts/api-coverage-drift-2026-05-08.md: typed Index variants miss var. Compute sample variance (ddof=1) over non-NAT nanoseconds in f64 and return Option<f64> (variance is unitless squared so f64 is the natural type). Single PR with regression tests.
close_reason: Shipped in a23f677: Datetime/Timedelta Index now expose pandas-parity var (sample, ddof=1). 1 regression test passes; 338 fp-index tests total.

Required output: add SeriesGroupBy::rolling(window) and a grouped rolling helper that applies existing Series rolling reductions per group, preserving the original flat Series index; expose sum/mean/min/max/std/count/var methods; add inline tests for per-group reset semantics and original index preservation; validate with scoped fp-frame test/check/clippy/fmt plus UBS on touched Rust. Parent umbrella: frankenpandas-nt65g.

title: Add PeriodIndex argmax/argmin/argsort
description: Reality-check finding artifacts/api-coverage-drift-2026-05-08.md: PeriodIndex misses argmax/argmin/argsort. Period derives Hash+Eq but not Ord; cmp_same_freq compares ordinals when frequencies match. Reject mixed-freq input. Single PR with regression tests.
close_reason: Shipped in faa7e30: PeriodIndex now exposes pandas-parity argmax/argmin/argsort with mixed-freq rejection. 1 regression test passes; 331 fp-index tests total.

title: Add TimedeltaIndex sum reduction
description: Reality-check finding artifacts/api-coverage-drift-2026-05-08.md: TimedeltaIndex misses sum. Sum non-NAT nanos in i128 then narrow. Returns Option<i64>. Single PR with regression test.
close_reason: Shipped in 5121b22: TimedeltaIndex now exposes pandas-parity sum via i128 accumulation. 1 regression test passes; 337 fp-index tests total.

title: Add DatetimeIndex tz_localize/tz_convert (UTC pass-through with reject-closed elsewhere)
description: Reality-check finding artifacts/api-coverage-drift-2026-05-08.md: DatetimeIndex misses tz_localize/tz_convert. fp-frame DataFrame already exposes these (br-6iac0) but the typed Index variant does not. FrankenPandas's underlying Datetime64 storage is naive UTC; full timezone metadata is deferred. Add as: tz_localize('UTC') returns clone (no-op pass-through since storage is already UTC), other timezones reject with a typed compatibility error referencing the deferred-tz roadmap. tz_convert always rejects since the source must be tz-aware. Single PR with regression tests covering: tz_localize('UTC') returns Self (clone), tz_localize('US/Eastern') rejects, tz_convert('UTC') rejects.
close_reason: Shipped in 70c19f1: DatetimeIndex now exposes pandas-parity tz_localize (UTC pass-through, others reject) and tz_convert (always rejects on tz-naive source). 1 regression test passes; 306 fp-index tests total.

title: Add typed Index variants reindex
description: Reality-check finding artifacts/api-coverage-drift-2026-05-08.md: typed Index variants miss reindex. Returns (target, indexer) where indexer is Vec<isize> with -1 for missing. Single PR with regression tests.
close_reason: Shipped in 014a346: 4 typed Index variants now expose pandas-parity reindex returning (target, indexer with -1 for missing). 1 regression test passes; 345 fp-index tests total.

title: Add DatetimeIndex is_month/quarter/year start/end boundary accessors
description:
Reality-check finding (artifacts/api-coverage-drift-2026-05-08.md): DatetimeIndex is missing six boundary-position accessors. Mirrors the day-of-X pattern shipped in br-k860x.

Add six pandas-named methods:
- is_month_start -> True when day == 1.
- is_month_end -> True when day == days_in_calendar_month.
- is_quarter_start -> True when month in {1,4,7,10} and day == 1.
- is_quarter_end -> True when month in {3,6,9,12} and day == days_in_calendar_month.
- is_year_start -> True when month == 1 and day == 1.
- is_year_end -> True when month == 12 and day == 31.

Each returns Vec<Option<bool>> aligned to DatetimeIndex labels via map_datetime_labels. Single PR. Add regression tests at every relevant boundary (Jan 1, Jan 31, Mar 31, Apr 1, Jun 30, Jul 1, Sep 30, Oct 1, Dec 31, Dec 32 → next year), Feb leap-year edge (Feb 29 in 2024 vs Feb 28 in 2023), and NAT propagation.
close_reason: Shipped in 6990193: DatetimeIndex now exposes pandas-parity is_month_start/is_month_end/is_quarter_start/is_quarter_end/is_year_start/is_year_end. 2 regression tests pass; 261 fp-index tests total.

title: Add Datetime/Timedelta Index get_indexer_non_unique
description: Reality-check finding artifacts/api-coverage-drift-2026-05-08.md: DatetimeIndex/TimedeltaIndex miss get_indexer_non_unique. Returns (Vec<isize>, Vec<usize>) — first is positions matching each target (multiple per target if duplicates), second is unmatched target ordinals. Single PR with regression tests.
close_reason: Shipped in 72228e2: Datetime/Timedelta Index now expose pandas-parity get_indexer_non_unique returning (positions, missing). 1 regression test passes; 325 fp-index tests total.

title: Add Categorical/Period Index where/putmask
description: Reality-check finding artifacts/api-coverage-drift-2026-05-08.md: CategoricalIndex/PeriodIndex miss the mask-based where/putmask methods. Add them for parity. Single PR with regression tests covering length-mismatch rejection.
close_reason: Shipped in 6cd1f90: PeriodIndex/CategoricalIndex now expose pandas-parity where/putmask with length-mismatch + freq/category-membership validation. 2 regression tests pass; 318 fp-index tests total.

title: Add Datetime/Timedelta/Period/Range Index searchsorted
description:
Reality-check finding (artifacts/api-coverage-drift-2026-05-08.md): the typed Index variants still miss searchsorted. Mirror the Index::searchsorted pattern (binary search with 'left' or 'right' side parameter).

Add one method on each of DatetimeIndex / TimedeltaIndex / PeriodIndex / RangeIndex:
- searchsorted(value, side) -> Result<usize, IndexError> — binary search returning the insertion position. side must be 'left' or 'right' (pandas semantics). NAT/Timedelta::NAT/Period values are passed through as the value type natively for each variant.

Single PR. Add regression tests covering: ascending Datetime ns lookup with both side modes, TimedeltaIndex lookup, PeriodIndex lookup with monthly periods, RangeIndex with positive step.
close_reason: Shipped in be322be: Datetime/Timedelta/Period/Range Index now expose pandas-parity searchsorted with side validation, NAT/Period-freq rejection, and descending-range rejection. 1 regression test exercises the matrix; 291 fp-index tests total.

title: Add Datetime/Timedelta Index asi8/strftime/microseconds/nanoseconds
description:
Reality-check finding (artifacts/api-coverage-drift-2026-05-08.md): IndexVariants is missing the underlying integer accessors (asi8) and the sub-day breakdown (microseconds/nanoseconds), plus DatetimeIndex.strftime.

Add five methods:
- DatetimeIndex.asi8 -> Vec<i64> — underlying nanoseconds-since-epoch with NAT preserved as i64::MIN.
- TimedeltaIndex.asi8 -> Vec<i64> — underlying nanosecond duration with NAT preserved as Timedelta::NAT.
- TimedeltaIndex.microseconds -> Vec<Option<i64>> — (nanos rem_euclid NANOS_PER_SEC) / 1_000 (0..=999_999).
- TimedeltaIndex.nanoseconds -> Vec<Option<i64>> — nanos rem_euclid 1_000 (0..=999).
- DatetimeIndex.strftime(format) -> Vec<Option<String>> — chrono format() applied per non-NAT label, NAT collapses to None.

Single PR. Add regression tests covering: asi8 round-trips, microseconds/nanoseconds for a (1 day + 2:34:56.789012345) input, NAT propagation, and strftime with %Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S%.3f format.
close_reason: Shipped in 8c7c856: DatetimeIndex now exposes pandas-parity asi8/strftime; TimedeltaIndex now exposes pandas-parity asi8/microseconds/nanoseconds. 3 regression tests pass; 267 fp-index tests total.

title: Add RangeIndex std/var/median closed-form
description: Reality-check finding artifacts/api-coverage-drift-2026-05-08.md: RangeIndex misses std/var/median. Compute via materialized values (n is small in practice). Single PR with regression test.
close_reason: Shipped in 304a916: RangeIndex now exposes pandas-parity median/var/std (sample, ddof=1). 1 regression test passes; 342 fp-index tests total.

title: [idea-wizard] build parity acceleration control plane

Goal: accelerate the existing FrankenPandas parity epics without duplicating their implementation work. The current open graph is mostly independent parity umbrellas (MultiIndex, SeriesGroupBy, Index variants, DataFrame wrappers, IO formats); this epic creates shared infrastructure and evidence workflows that make those epics easier to drain correctly.

Top winnowed ideas:
1. Live pandas API coverage gate plus bead drift reconciler.
2. Differential fixture generator with RaptorQ sidecars for new method slices.
3. AACE semantic witness ledger for index-alignment/materialization boundaries.
4. High-RAM benchmark baseline and execution telemetry for pandas-vs-Franken comparisons.
5. Bead hygiene/progress reconciler for stale umbrella descriptions and child closeout state.
6. Null/NaN metamorphic conformance suite across alignment/groupby/join/reshape.
7. Error compatibility catalog and harness for pandas-observable exceptions.

Acceptance: all children remain non-overlapping with open implementation epics, each child names concrete output artifacts, and bv reports no dependency cycles after creation.

Required output:

Validation: run the scanner, run the focused test/golden update path, run cargo fmt --check if Rust files change, run rch exec -- cargo test for the owning crate, and run ubs on touched Rust files.

Single PR with regression tests covering start/middle/end insertion, OOB rejection, name preservation.

title: Add TimedeltaIndex argmax/argmin/argsort/unique/factorize/value_counts/duplicated/drop_duplicates/dropna
description:
Reality-check finding (artifacts/api-coverage-drift-2026-05-08.md): TimedeltaIndex needs the same forwarder/skipna surface that br-z9guv just added on DatetimeIndex.

Add nine methods on TimedeltaIndex:
- argmax / argmin -> walk labels left-to-right, skip Timedelta::NAT, return the *first* tied position; empty / all-NAT raises pandas-style 'attempt to get argmax/argmin of an empty sequence' via IndexError::InvalidArgument.
- argsort -> forwards to Index::argsort.
- unique -> forwards to Index::unique then rebuilds as TimedeltaIndex.
- factorize -> forwards to Index::factorize. NAT inputs receive -1 codes; uniques excludes NAT.
- value_counts -> forwards to Index::value_counts (dropna=true default).
- duplicated -> forwards.
- drop_duplicates -> forwards and rebuilds as TimedeltaIndex.
- dropna -> filters out Timedelta::NAT sentinels while preserving the index name.

Single PR. Add regression tests covering: a 6-label TimedeltaIndex with duplicates + NAT exercising every branch, empty-sequence rejection for argmax/argmin, and dropna preserving the index name.
close_reason: Shipped in a42cd4e: TimedeltaIndex now exposes pandas-parity argmax/argmin (skipna+first-tied), argsort, unique, factorize (NAT->-1), value_counts (dropna), duplicated, drop_duplicates, dropna. 3 regression tests pass; 273 fp-index tests total.
